mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, lifting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls confirm a demand-driven expansion with income validation; the interaction produces stronger positive S&P 500 forward returns than either series in isolation.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month increases in both RSAFS and PAYEMS
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform the simple PAYEMS-only signal in directional accuracy over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are positive in less than 55% of joint-up months.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in housing starts signals that interest-rate-sensitive demand is rolling over, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations reset lower.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months during identified late-cycle periods, or the signal's Information Coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds trailing CPI inflation, restrictive real monetary policy suppresses nominal demand and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in > 60% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds trailing CPI growth, or the spread fails to correlate negatively with subsequent returns (Rank IC > -0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a contraction in goods-sector output that precedes negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is not significantly negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above its 4-quarter rolling average signals sustained above-trend aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following above-trend GDP growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) compared to below-trend periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected month-over-month cut in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate and lowers corporate borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months, or the FEDFUNDS change fails to exhibit positive Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window.
